key_insights: New 235-line module implementing terminal capability detection
TermLevel enum derives Ord/PartialOrd for natural capability ordering (Dumb < Basic < Extended < Full < TrueColor)
Pure classification logic in classify_level() function isolated from environment I/O, enabling deterministic unit testing
Width detection via terminal_size crate, re-queried on every TermCaps::detect() call (never cached) to handle Zellij pane resizes
Two-column layout threshold hardcoded as width ≥ 100 characters
No version constraints; uses default caret (^0.4) SemVer
Registered mod termcaps; in module list (alphabetically ordered after serve, before util)
No pub/private exports, internal module only (not exposed in public API yet)

patterns_discovered.description: ZELLIJ (presence only) > COLORTERM (exact value match) > TERM (prefix/contains match) > default fallback
patterns_discovered.rationale: Zellij always provides Full+ support; COLORTERM is explicit intent; TERM is terminal emulator class; fallback is conservative (Basic not Dumb)

patterns_discovered.description: classify_level(zellij, colorterm, term) takes env vars as parameters, never calls std::env::var() internally
patterns_discovered.rationale: Enables 100% unit test coverage without environment mutation; detect_level() wraps it with I/O

patterns_discovered.pattern: Lazy capability level + eager width detection
patterns_discovered.description: TermLevel is static per session, but terminal_size ioctl is called on every TermCaps::detect()
patterns_discovered.rationale: Supports dynamic pane resizing in Zellij without re-detecting color support; width changes faster than terminal type

patterns_discovered.description: TermLevel implements Ord/PartialOrd, allows comparisons like level >= TermLevel::Full
patterns_discovered.rationale: Cleaner conditional logic for feature gates (e.g., 'if caps.level >= Full { use unicode }')

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: TERM=empty should be Dumb, not Basic
gotchas_discovered.context: Classification order: checks term.is_empty() before fallback to Basic
gotchas_discovered.impact: Prevents misclassification of bare shells (no TERM) as Basic (8-color)

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Zellij detection is presence-only (Some vs None), not value inspection
gotchas_discovered.impact: Avoids brittle string matching on ZELLIJ value; any non-empty value indicates Zellij session

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Width detection does NOT fall back to Extended level if ioctl fails
gotchas_discovered.context: terminal_size().map_or(80, ...) — hardcoded 80 as default, independent of TermLevel
gotchas_discovered.impact: Safe default (80 char terminals are ubiquitous); but two_column will be false, not derived from level

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: two_column is a computed field in struct, not stored in enum
gotchas_discovered.impact: Ensures consistency; but two_column threshold (100) is magic number, not parameterized

approach_details: ✓ Added terminal_size 0.4 dependency with no compilation issues
✓ Implemented all 5 TermLevel variants with correct ordering semantics
✓ Detection logic follows exact priority order specified (ZELLIJ→COLORTERM→TERM→dumb→Basic)
✓ Pure classification function enabled 18 unit tests covering priority ordering, edge cases, and level comparisons
✓ Width re-queried on every detect() call for Zellij pane resize support
✓ Module registered in main.rs and compiles without warnings
✓ Comprehensive doc comments explain detection order and caching strategy

recommendations.suggestion: Cache TERM/COLORTERM detection but re-query width on every render. Rationale: env var reads are cheap but terminal_size ioctl is slower; caching level reduces redundant classification on each frame.

Integrate terminfo database for TERM lookups instead of hardcoded xterm/screen/tmux/rxvt. This would support obscure terminals and custom TERM aliases. Reference: ncurses terminfo, Rust termion crate.
Add FORCE_TERM / NO_COLOR env vars for CLI testing and accessibility. Example: FORCE_TERM=dumb bop ui would override all detection.
Add TermCaps usage example in rendering module (once TUI is implemented). Document that width must be re-queried after resize events.
Spec 029 (bop ui) will consume TermCaps for layout selection. When implementing kanban TUI, create layout traits parametrized by TermLevel (e.g., trait DumbLayout, trait TrueColorLayout).
